title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Assumption Clinic
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an inference and limitations brief and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Regression Diagnostics evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Regression Diagnostics assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Inspect residuals, leverage, confounding, and extrapolation before regression claims. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math intermediate statistics inference. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Regression Diagnostics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an inference and limitations brief.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Regression software validates causal conclusions automatically. This fails the Regression Diagnostics contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: The observed values are 11, 15, 19, 15 minutes. Calculate their mean.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes minutes or min.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Regression Diagnostics applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect residuals, leverage, confounding, and extrapolation before regression claims.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Study Design to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Study Design.

checks.description: The decision applies Confidence Intervals to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Confidence Intervals.

checks.description: The decision applies Hypothesis Tests to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Hypothesis Tests.

checks.description: The decision applies Regression Diagnostics to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Regression Diagnostics.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using inspect and residuals plus specific case evidence.
